title: Taste signal
description: Structured output contract for hooks/scripts/update-taste.mjs. Describes a single signal (reject/approve/neutral/mixed) extracted from a user prompt, plus the scope it applies to. Used by the skill's style-selection stage to bias the candidate pool for future generations.

signal.description: Classification of the user's response. 'mixed' = both reject and approve cues detected in the same prompt; 'neutral' = no cues found (the hook should not persist a neutral signal).

scope.description: Which dimension of the taste profile the signal applies to. 'global' = applies to the entire generation (not a single axis).

evidence_quote.description: Short verbatim quote from the user prompt that triggered the classification. Quote is normalised (whitespace collapsed, markdown structural chars stripped) to keep system.md readable.

target_id.description: Style id (when scope='style'), palette id, or other identifier the signal pins to. Optional — not every signal has a named target.

detected_at.description: ISO-8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) for when the signal was recorded. Used to timestamp the entry in system.md.
